note: Gnostic sacred order normalized onto the 21-agent spine. Primary array: the 30 aeons of the Valentinian Pleroma, emanated as syzygies (pairs) in three groups - the Ogdoad (8), the Decad (10) and the Dodecad (12), culminating in Sophia whose fall and restoration drive the drama of salvation. Secondary lattices: the seven Archons of the Hebdomad (the planetary rulers and Demiurge order that mirror and parody the seven heavens), the three natures of Valentinian anthropology (hylic, psychic, pneumatic), and the 365 heavens of Abraxas from the system of Basilides. The 30 aeons cover all 21 agents; the 7 archons bridge to the seven planets (matrix 24) and the seven lower chakras; the three natures bridge to the three gunas.
aeon_note: The Pleroma unfolds as syzygies: Bythos and Sige beget Nous and Aletheia, who beget Logos and Zoe, who beget Anthropos and Ecclesia - the First Ogdoad. From Logos and Zoe issue the Decad; from Anthropos and Ecclesia the Dodecad. The number 30 also mirrors the hidden years of Christ before his baptism. Names of the Decad and Dodecad follow Irenaeus and Tertullian (Adversus Valentinianos); some traditions count Bythos outside the thirty and add Horos (the Limit) - here the canonical thirty are used.
mera_axis: 9-dimensional axis (mera 1-9): chakras 1-7 (Muladhara..Sahasrara) + Monada (8) + Absolut (9); each spine agent carries its mera.

archons.role: The blind arrogant Demiurge (Saklas, Samael); chief Archon and false creator of the material world.
Ruler of the sphere of Jupiter; counterfeit authority and dominion.
Lord of hosts, sphere of Mars; the archon said to repent and ascend.
Ruler of the sphere of Venus.
Ruler of the sphere of the Sun.
Ruler of the sphere of Mercury.
Ruler of the sphere of the Moon; outermost gate of the soul ascent.

three_natures.role: Material nature bound to matter; the carnal type.
Animate soul-nature capable of choice; the type that may be saved by works and faith.
Spiritual seed, the divine spark destined for the Pleroma.

abraxas.name: Abraxas (Abrasax)
abraxas.role: The Great Archon (megas archon) in the system of Basilides; ruler of the 365 heavens, one for each day of the solar year. The Greek letters of the name sum to 365 (A-1, B-2, R-100, A-1, X-60, A-1, S-200). In the Nag Hammadi texts Abraxas appears instead as a salvific aeon.
abraxas.note: The 365 heavens descend in graded emanations; the lowest heaven, ruled by the god of this world, fashioned matter. The individual heavens are not enumerated by name in the sources.

svet_ra.note: Bythos the unfathomable Source and Ainos the praise that returns to the Father - origin and crown of the Pleroma.

iskra.note: Elpis - Hope, the spark that reaches across the deficiency toward the light.

brahma.note: Nous and Autophyes - the self-existent creative Mind, first-born intellect of the Father.

vishnu.note: Ecclesia and Ecclesiasticus - the divine Assembly that preserves communion.

ananda.note: Hedone and Macaria - Delight and Happiness, the bliss of the fullness.

shanti.note: Sige and Ageratos - Silence and the Ageless, the unmoving peace of the Source.

jnana.note: Aletheia and Synesis - Truth and Understanding, the gnosis of the Pleroma.

shiva.note: Bythios - the fathomless deep, dissolution at the edge of the abyss.

tejas.note: Monogenes and Theletos - the Only-begotten and the Perfect, radiant completion.

sarasvati.note: Logos - the Word that articulates the silent Depth into speech.

akasha.note: Anthropos - the divine Humanity, the archetypal Man as cosmic space.

prema.note: Henosis and Agape - Union and Love, the binding affection of the syzygies.

parvati.note: Paracletus and Metricos - the Comforter and the Maternal, care within the fullness.

vayu.note: Zoe - Life, the living breath flowing from the Word.

shakti.note: Sophia - Wisdom, the active power whose longing precipitates the fall and restoration.

agni.note: Syncrasis - the commixture, the refining blend of qualities.

varuna.note: Mixis - the mingling of waters, the flowing commingling of aeons.

lakshmi.note: Macariotes - Blessedness, the abundance of the fullness.

prithvi.note: Acinetos - the Immovable, the steadfast ground of the fullness.

dharma.note: Pistis - Faith, the firm trust that orders the fullness.

karma.note: Patricos - the Paternal, the lawful cause descending through the generations.

sources: Irenaeus - Against Heresies I.1-8 (the thirty Aeons)
Tertullian - Adversus Valentinianos VII-VIII (names of the Decad and Dodecad)
Apocryphon of John (Secret Book of John) - the Archons and Yaldabaoth
gnosis.org/library/valentinus - Brief Summary of Valentinian Theology
